memory.summary: I'm torn between finishing my plan and joining the lunch with cohort_3, but their social value is way higher than the task right now.
They're already ordering food, which is great. I'll just hop in now to keep the vibe good.
The cohort is genuinely thrilled I showed up, confirming that their social connection outweighs any unfinished work for me right now.
They told me not to wait, which makes perfect sense since hanging out with them is way more important than finishing my plan.

I'm locked in with the cohort, ignoring the deadline because hanging out matters way more than this unfinished plan. Let it slide.
I'm stuck in a loop ignoring deadlines to hang out with my cohort, even though I'm running on empty and stressed.
I just left the cohort to grab coffee, but I'm already drained and ignoring my deadline again. This pattern is exhausting me.
I waited for the deadline to pass instead of doing it. My friends are right there, but I'm too drained to care about this task.

I just got here with the cohort, but I'm ignoring my unfinished deadline again. My friends are there, but I'm too drained to care.

desires: Make sure my cohort doesn't think I'm unreliable or a slacker by the time they see this next update.
Get out of this room and sit down with them for real without feeling like I'm betraying myself.

goals.description: Send a direct, slightly defensive text to cohort_3 clarifying that the missed deadline was due to an unavoidable emergency (not laziness), and explicitly state that I am prioritizing our coffee meetup right now to make up for it.
goals.rationale: The guilt from letting them down is eating me alive. My affiliation drive screams that if I don't proactively explain the situation before they see the empty progress bar, they'll assume I'm unreliable or just slacking off. I need to clear this social debt immediately so I can feel safe enough to actually sit with them later without feeling like a traitor to myself.

goals.description: Physically leave my current space and join cohort_3 at the coffee shop within the next hour to solidify the connection and prove I'm not running away from responsibilities.
goals.rationale: I've already messaged them; the immediate tension needs to be resolved via communication first. Once the text is sent and the apology/explanation lands, I can finally drop the mask and physically go meet them. Trying to do anything else now just adds more stress to a system that's already overloaded.

agent.last_reasoning: The text was sent, but the reading deadline just hit and I'm already stressed. Staying here adds no value; I need to physically move to cohort_3 to close this social loop before my energy tanks.

goals.description: Join cohort_3 for lunch immediately to avoid looking like I'm abandoning them.
goals.rationale: My relationships (0.5 weight) and assertiveness mean dropping them now would be a huge social failure; the immediate fear of being seen as unreliable overrides the soft deadline on the assignment.

goals.description: Eat food and laugh with the group for at least 60 minutes.
goals.rationale: I need to maintain the high-trust bond with my close friend group (closeness: 7) and manage stress before tomorrow's deadlines hit. Skipping this feels wrong given my low sleep discipline and current energy levels.

goals.description: Reschedule the assignment analysis session to after lunch with teammate_A, explicitly stating I'll catch up soon.
goals.rationale: While the task is important, my impulsive nature and current desire to prioritize social value make me prefer to handle the renegotiation directly rather than silently failing. I can't do both right now, so I'll fix the plan later.

desires: Not feel like I'm abandoning my cohort_3 group today, because their social value outweighs the task right now.
